_meta.note: All pesticide recommendations follow CIB&RC (Central Insecticides Board & Registration Committee) approved labels. Dosages are per-litre of water unless stated as per-hectare.
_meta.how_to_use: Keys match the output of parse_class_name() in vision_agent.py. Look up by 'crop___disease' (lowercase, underscores). Diagnosis agent queries this before calling Claude.

schema.severity_on_detection: how severe is this disease when visually obvious
schema.yield_loss_pct_max: maximum yield loss if completely untreated

Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to brown scabby lesions on leaves and fruit surface. Leaves may curl and drop early.
Apple___Apple_scab.conditions_favoring: Cool, wet spring weather; temperatures 18–24°C with leaf wetness for 9+ hours

organic_treatment.preparation: Wettable sulfur 80% WP @ 3g per litre of water
organic_treatment.notes: Apply before rain if forecast. Do not apply when temperature >32°C — causes phytotoxicity.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays at 10–15 day intervals

Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Plant resistant varieties (Florina, Liberty). Rake and destroy fallen leaves. Prune for air circulation.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Circular brown to black lesions on fruit starting at the calyx end. Leaf spots with purple border and tan center (frogeye leaf spot).
Apple___Black_rot.conditions_favoring: Warm humid weather, temperatures 20–30°C. Enters through wounds.

organic_treatment.preparation: Bordeaux mixture 1% (10g copper sulphate + 10g lime in 1 litre water)
organic_treatment.notes: Remove and destroy all mummified fruit immediately.

primary.frequency: 3 sprays at 10-day interval starting at early fruit development
primary.icar_ref: ICAR Crop Protection Compendium, Apple Fungal Diseases

Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Prune dead wood in winter. Remove mummified fruit before bud break. Avoid wounding fruit.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface in spring/early summer. Lesions may develop tube-like projections on undersides.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.conditions_favoring: Requires alternating hosts (juniper/cedar nearby). Spores spread during wet spring.

organic_treatment.preparation: Wettable sulfur 80% WP @ 3g per litre
organic_treatment.frequency: At pink bud stage, petal fall, and 10 days after
organic_treatment.notes: Remove any nearby juniper/cedar trees if possible.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays from pink bud to 3 weeks after petal fall

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Plant rust-resistant apple varieties. Remove alternate juniper hosts within 300m if feasible.

organic_treatment.notes: Continue regular preventive care.

Apple___healthy.prevention: Maintain orchard hygiene. Annual dormant spray with lime sulfur as preventive measure.

organic_treatment.notes: Maintain soil pH 4.5–5.5 for healthy blueberry growth.

Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Ensure acidic soil. Prune for airflow. Mulch with pine bark.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on young leaves, shoots, and fruit surface. Leaves may curl and distort.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.conditions_favoring: Warm dry days (20–28°C) with cool nights. High humidity without rain.

organic_treatment.preparation: Potassium bicarbonate 5g per litre OR neem oil 5ml + 1ml soap per litre
organic_treatment.notes: Apply early morning or evening. Neem oil effective at early stages.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays at 14-day intervals
primary.icar_ref: ICAR Crop Protection Guidelines, Stone Fruits

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Avoid dense planting. Prune for airflow. Avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Annual dormant copper spray. Good drainage.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular tan to gray lesions parallel to leaf veins. Lesions have well-defined borders. Severe infection causes entire leaf death.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.conditions_favoring: High humidity (>90%), warm temperatures 25–30°C, poor air circulation in dense stands.

organic_treatment.preparation: No direct organic spray — cultural control is most effective
organic_treatment.frequency: Preventive — rotate with non-cereal crop next season
organic_treatment.notes: Remove and destroy infected crop debris after harvest.

primary.frequency: 2 sprays — at silking and 2 weeks after

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Increase row spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small, round to elongated, brick-red pustules on both leaf surfaces. Pustules turn black as season progresses.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.conditions_favoring: Cool temperatures 16–23°C with high humidity. Spores spread by wind.

organic_treatment.preparation: Dust sulfur 5–6 kg per hectare
organic_treatment.notes: Best applied early morning before dew dries.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays at 10-day intervals starting at first sign

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Use rust-resistant varieties. Avoid late planting. Ensure good field drainage.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long, cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions on leaves, 2.5–15cm long. Lesions may cover entire leaf in severe cases.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.conditions_favoring: Temperatures 18–27°C, humid conditions, extended leaf wetness.

organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + liquid soap 1ml per litre water
organic_treatment.notes: Effective only at early stage. Remove badly infected lower leaves.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays at 10–14 day intervals

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Crop rotation with non-grass crops. Plant resistant hybrids (check with local KVK for regional varieties). Destroy crop debris after harvest.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Balanced fertilization. Ensure proper drainage. Rotate crops every 2 years.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Small, circular tan spots with dark brown borders on leaves. Infected berries shrivel into hard black mummies.
Grape___Black_rot.conditions_favoring: Warm wet weather 24–29°C. Spores released during rain.

organic_treatment.preparation: 1% Bordeaux mixture (10g copper sulphate + 10g lime per litre water)
organic_treatment.frequency: At bud break, before bloom, after bloom, and at berry set (4 sprays total)
organic_treatment.notes: Remove and destroy all mummified berries from the vineyard.

primary.frequency: 4–6 sprays through the season at 10–14 day intervals

Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Remove mummified fruit and infected leaves. Prune for open canopy. Avoid wetting foliage when irrigating.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Tiger-striped yellowing/browning between leaf veins. Berries develop dark purple spots (measles). In severe cases, sudden whole-vine wilting (apoplexy).
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).conditions_favoring: Enters through pruning wounds. Dry summers after wet winters.

organic_treatment.preparation: Apply Trichoderma-based biocontrol paste (Trichoderma viride 1% WP mixed with water to paste) on all pruning cuts immediately after pruning
organic_treatment.notes: No curative spray exists. Focus entirely on prevention via wound protection.

primary.dose: 2g per litre — paint onto pruning cuts within 24 hours
primary.frequency: Every pruning season

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Prune during dry weather. Apply wound sealant immediately. Remove and destroy severely infected vines. Avoid large pruning wounds.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Angular brown spots on leaves surrounded by yellow halo. Spots enlarge and leaf dies. Fruit may also be affected.

organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + liquid soap 1ml per litre water
organic_treatment.notes: Remove and dispose infected leaves.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays at 15-day intervals

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Avoid dense canopy. Ensure good drainage. Remove fallen leaves.

Grape___healthy.prevention: Annual pruning. Dormant copper spray. Weed management in vineyard.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Asymmetric yellowing of leaves (blotchy mottle). Fruit small, lopsided, bitter, with green tip even when ripe. Tree decline over 2–5 years.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).conditions_favoring: Spread by Asian citrus psyllid (Diaphorina citri). Warm humid conditions favor psyllid population.

organic_treatment.preparation: No cure exists. Neem oil 5ml + soap 1ml per litre to control psyllid vector
organic_treatment.frequency: Psyllid spray every 2 weeks during new flush period
organic_treatment.notes: This disease has NO CURE. Infected trees must be removed and destroyed to protect neighboring trees. Inform your local horticulture department immediately.

primary.pesticide: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (for psyllid vector control only)
primary.frequency: At new flush emergence (3–4 times per year)

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free planting material. Install psyllid monitoring traps. Report suspected cases to State Horticulture Department. Quarantine new plants before introducing to orchard.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).government_scheme: Infected citrus growers may be eligible for compensation under MIDH scheme — contact nearest horticulture officer

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown with purple border. Spots may fall out creating 'shot-hole' appearance. Fruit shows sunken dark spots.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.conditions_favoring: Wet spring weather, strong winds that cause wounds, temperatures 19–28°C.

organic_treatment.preparation: Copper hydroxide 53.8% DF @ 2.5g per litre
organic_treatment.frequency: Preventive sprays — at green tip, pink bud, petal fall
organic_treatment.notes: Avoid spraying during bloom to protect bees.

primary.frequency: 3–4 sprays from bud swell through shuck split

Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant in well-drained site with windbreaks. Use resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Peach___healthy.prevention: Dormant copper spray. Proper pruning. Remove fallen fruit.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked spots on leaves that become brown with yellow halo. Fruit shows raised, rough, brown to black spots.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.conditions_favoring: Warm rainy weather 24–30°C. Spreads through rain splash and wind.

organic_treatment.preparation: Copper hydroxide 53.8% @ 2g per litre OR Bordeaux mixture 1%
organic_treatment.notes: Use disease-free certified seed. Avoid working in field when wet.

primary.frequency: 4–6 sprays at 7–10 day intervals

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Crop rotation — avoid solanaceous crops in same plot for 3 years. Drip irrigation instead of overhead.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Rotate crops. Use certified seed. Balanced nutrition especially calcium.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown to black lesions with concentric rings (target board pattern) on older leaves. Yellowing around spots. Severe infection causes defoliation.
Potato___Early_blight.conditions_favoring: Temperatures 24–29°C, alternating wet and dry conditions. Weakened plants more susceptible.

organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + liquid soap 1ml per litre of water
organic_treatment.notes: Remove and burn severely infected lower leaves. Avoid wetting foliage.

primary.frequency: 3–4 sprays at 10-day intervals starting at first symptom
primary.icar_ref: ICAR-CPRI Potato Disease Management Bulletin No. 8

Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Use disease-free certified seed. Avoid water stress. Balanced fertilization — excess nitrogen worsens disease. Destroy crop debris.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaf margins/tips that rapidly turn dark brown. White cottony fungal growth on underside in humid weather. Foul smell from infected tubers.
Potato___Late_blight.conditions_favoring: Cool (15–20°C) and very wet conditions. Can destroy entire crop in 7–10 days under ideal conditions.

organic_treatment.preparation: 1% Bordeaux mixture (10g copper sulphate + 10g lime per litre water)
organic_treatment.frequency: Preventive only — every 7 days during cool/wet weather forecasts
organic_treatment.notes: CRITICAL: Late blight spreads extremely rapidly. Do NOT wait — treat the moment symptoms appear. Bordeaux mixture is only preventive; use chemicals for active infection.

primary.frequency: Every 7 days until conditions improve (min 3 sprays)
primary.icar_ref: ICAR-CPRI Late Blight Emergency Advisory, Bulletin No. 12

alternative.frequency: Every 7 days, alternate with primary to prevent resistance

Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Bahar, Kufri Jyoti). Ensure good drainage. Avoid planting in low-lying fields. Destroy volunteer potato plants.
Potato___Late_blight.government_scheme: PMFBY crop insurance covers late blight losses — contact your bank or CSC center

Potato___healthy.prevention: Use certified seed. Preventive Bordeaux spray during cool wet weather. Proper earthing-up.

Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Annual cane pruning. Good air circulation. Remove old canes after harvest.

Soybean___healthy.prevention: Seed treatment with Rhizobium + Trichoderma. Crop rotation. Avoid waterlogging.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery spots on upper leaf surface, starting as circular patches. Spreads to cover entire leaf. Infected leaves yellow and die prematurely.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.conditions_favoring: Warm dry days (23–30°C), high humidity at night, no rain to wash spores.

organic_treatment.method: Baking soda or neem oil spray
organic_treatment.preparation: Baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) 10g + few drops soap per litre water OR neem oil 5ml per litre
organic_treatment.notes: Spray both leaf surfaces. Early morning application is most effective.

primary.pesticide: Sulfur 40% WG or wettable sulfur 80% WP
primary.frequency: Every 10–14 days, 2–3 sprays

Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure good spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation in evening.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Small dark purple to reddish-purple irregular spots on leaf surfaces. Centers turn grayish and spots may coalesce. Severely infected leaves wither (scorch).
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.conditions_favoring: Wet, humid conditions. Cool to warm temperatures 20–28°C.

organic_treatment.preparation: Copper oxychloride 50% WP @ 3g per litre
organic_treatment.notes: Remove and destroy old infected leaves before new growth.

primary.frequency: 3–4 sprays at 10–14 day intervals

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Use certified disease-free runners. Avoid wetting foliage. Remove old leaves during renovation.

Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Use disease-free runners. Mulch to prevent soil splash. Remove old leaves monthly.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked circular spots on leaves, 3–5mm diameter. Spots turn dark brown with yellow halo. Fruit shows raised, rough, dark spots that crack.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.conditions_favoring: Warm (24–30°C), wet, and windy conditions. Spreads rapidly by rain splash.

organic_treatment.preparation: Copper oxychloride 50% WP @ 3g per litre
organic_treatment.frequency: Every 7–10 days during wet weather, 4–5 sprays
organic_treatment.notes: Use only certified disease-free seed or transplants.

primary.frequency: 4–6 sprays at 7-day intervals

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Crop rotation (3 years away from solanaceous crops). Use drip irrigation. Remove infected plant debris immediately.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown circular lesions with concentric rings (target-board pattern) on older lower leaves. Yellow halo around spots. Stem lesions at soil level (collar rot in seedlings).
Tomato___Early_blight.conditions_favoring: Warm temperatures 24–29°C, alternating wet/dry, nutrient-deficient plants.

organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + copper oxychloride 2g + liquid soap 1ml per litre water
organic_treatment.notes: Remove infected lower leaves before spraying. Mulch around plants.

primary.frequency: 4–5 sprays at 7–10 day intervals
primary.icar_ref: ICAR-IIHR Tomato IPM Package, Bulletin No. 14

Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties (Arka Rakshak, Arka Samrat). Avoid overhead irrigation. Balance fertilizer — excess N worsens disease.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Greasy water-soaked patches on leaves that rapidly turn dark brown/black. White cottony growth on underside in humid conditions. Dark streaks on stems. Brown discoloration of fruit.
Tomato___Late_blight.conditions_favoring: Cool (15–20°C) and wet. Can destroy crop within 1 week under favorable conditions.

organic_treatment.preparation: 1% Bordeaux mixture every 7 days when cool/wet weather is forecast
organic_treatment.frequency: Preventive only — must also use chemical treatment for active infection
organic_treatment.notes: EMERGENCY: Late blight can destroy entire crop in 7–10 days. Begin chemical treatment immediately — do not wait.

primary.pesticide: Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP (Curzate M8 or equivalent)
primary.frequency: Every 5–7 days, minimum 3 sprays

alternative.frequency: Every 7 days — alternate with primary to prevent resistance

Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid planting near potatoes. Do not wet foliage. Ensure good drainage. Remove and burn infected plants immediately.
Tomato___Late_blight.government_scheme: PMFBY covers late blight losses — contact your bank or Common Service Center

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale greenish-yellow spots on upper leaf surface. Olive-green to grayish-purple velvety growth on underside. Infected leaves curl, wither, and drop.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.conditions_favoring: High humidity (>85%), temperatures 22–27°C. Common in protected cultivation (polyhouses).

organic_treatment.preparation: Wettable sulfur 80% WP @ 3g per litre
organic_treatment.notes: Increase ventilation in polyhouses. Reduce humidity by improving air circulation.

primary.frequency: 3–4 sprays at 10-day intervals

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Reduce polyhouse humidity with ventilation. Avoid overhead watering. Remove infected leaves.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Numerous small (3–6mm) circular spots with dark brown border and tan/grey center. Tiny black pycnidia (fruiting bodies) visible in spot centers. Lower leaves affected first.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.conditions_favoring: Wet, warm (20–25°C) conditions. Spreads rapidly during rainy seasons.

organic_treatment.preparation: Copper oxychloride 50% WP @ 3g per litre
organic_treatment.notes: Start spraying from transplanting. Remove badly infected lower leaves.

primary.frequency: 5–6 sprays at 7–10 day intervals starting from early vegetative stage

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: 3-year crop rotation. Remove infected lower leaves. Mulch to prevent soil splash. Stake plants for good air circulation.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Fine yellow stippling on upper leaf surface. Underside has fine webbing and tiny mites visible. Leaves turn bronze/brown and die in severe infestations.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.conditions_favoring: Hot, dry conditions (above 30°C, low humidity). Dusty conditions. Stressed plants.

organic_treatment.method: Neem oil spray + strong water jet
organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + liquid soap 1ml per litre water
organic_treatment.notes: First wash plants with strong water jet (especially undersides) to dislodge mites. Spray neem oil immediately after. Release predatory mite Phytoseiulus persimilis if available.

primary.frequency: 2 sprays at 7-day interval. Rotate with different chemical next generation.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Maintain soil moisture to reduce plant stress. Avoid dusty conditions. Control nearby weeds. Avoid excessive nitrogen.

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Dark brown to black spots with concentric rings (target-like pattern). Spots have yellow halo. Unlike early blight, occurs on all-aged leaves including younger ones.
Tomato___Target_Spot.conditions_favoring: Warm humid conditions 24–30°C, extended leaf wetness.

organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + liquid soap 1ml per litre water
organic_treatment.notes: Remove infected leaves. Improve air circulation.

primary.frequency: 2–3 sprays at 14-day intervals

Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Crop rotation. Avoid dense planting. Remove lower leaves touching soil.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.hindi_name: टमाटर का पीला पत्ती मरोड़ वायरस
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Severe upward curling and yellowing of young leaves. Stunted plant growth. Leaves become small and leathery. Little to no fruit set.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.conditions_favoring: High whitefly (Bemisia tabaci) population. Hot dry conditions favor vector. Most damaging in early crop stages.

organic_treatment.preparation: Neem oil 5ml + liquid soap 1ml per litre for whitefly. Use yellow sticky traps (30/acre).
organic_treatment.frequency: Neem spray every 5 days. Replace sticky traps every 2 weeks.
organic_treatment.notes: NO CURE for viral infection. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Focus entirely on whitefly vector control in surrounding healthy plants.

primary.dose: 0.5ml per litre of water as foliar OR 2ml per litre as drench
primary.frequency: Every 7–10 days — focus on young plants before infection

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use virus-resistant varieties (Arka Rakshak, Arka Samrat — IIHR released). Install 50-mesh insect-proof nets on nursery. Spray imidacloprid on transplants before field planting. Install reflective silver mulch.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.government_scheme: Virus-resistant variety seeds available at subsidized rates through state horticulture department

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves. Leaves may be distorted, narrow, or fernleaf-shaped. Fruit may show yellow rings. Stunted growth.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.conditions_favoring: Spreads mechanically — hands, tools, clothing. Very stable virus, survives in plant debris for long periods.

organic_treatment.preparation: Wash hands with soap before handling plants. Dip tools in 10% bleach solution (100ml bleach + 900ml water) for 30 seconds between plants.
organic_treatment.notes: NO CURE. Remove and destroy infected plants. Do NOT compost infected material. Do NOT smoke near tomato plants (tobacco carries related virus).

primary.pesticide: No effective chemical treatment for virus itself
primary.frequency: N/A — focus on sanitation and roguing
primary.icar_ref: ICAR-IIHR Viral Disease Management in Tomato

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use virus-free certified seed or transplants. Resistant varieties. Disinfect tools. Remove infected plants early. Do not handle plants when wet.

Tomato___healthy.prevention: Weekly scouting. Balanced fertilization (avoid excess N). Drip irrigation. Crop rotation.

Wheat___Leaf_rust.symptoms: Small, circular to oval orange-brown pustules scattered on upper leaf surface. Pustules contain orange-brown powdery spores. Severe infection causes leaf shriveling.
Wheat___Leaf_rust.conditions_favoring: Temperatures 15–22°C, high humidity, dew on leaves. Spores spread by wind over long distances.

organic_treatment.preparation: Dust sulfur 20 kg per hectare
organic_treatment.frequency: At first sign — preventive dusting most effective
organic_treatment.notes: Cultural control: Early sowing of resistant varieties. Sulfur is mainly preventive — use fungicide for active infection.

primary.frequency: 1–2 sprays at 10–14 day interval if disease spreads above 5% leaf area
primary.icar_ref: ICAR-IIWBR (Directorate of Wheat Research) Rust Management Bulletin 2024

Wheat___Leaf_rust.prevention: Plant rust-resistant varieties (HD 3086, DBW 187, WH 1105). Early sowing (Oct–Nov). Destroy volunteer wheat after harvest. Monitor regularly from tillering stage.
Wheat___Leaf_rust.government_scheme: ICAR-IIWBR issues real-time rust alerts — check wheatrust.res.in for regional advisories

Wheat___Stripe_rust.symptoms: Yellow to orange stripe-like lines of pustules parallel to leaf veins. Leaves turn completely yellow in severe cases. Young leaves most affected.
Wheat___Stripe_rust.conditions_favoring: Cool temperatures 10–15°C with moisture. More common in hilly/northern regions. Can spread explosively.

organic_treatment.preparation: Dust sulfur 20–25 kg per hectare
organic_treatment.notes: CRITICAL: Stripe rust is more aggressive than leaf rust. In cool weather it can destroy entire field in 2–3 weeks. Report any sudden stripe rust outbreak to your nearest KVK or ICAR-IIWBR.

primary.frequency: 1–2 sprays — single timely spray can save entire crop

Wheat___Stripe_rust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties (HD 3226, DBW 222). Early sowing. Avoid late crop maturity during spring when conditions favor rust. Subscribe to ICAR wheat rust SMS alert service.
Wheat___Stripe_rust.government_scheme: For varieties to grow, check state agriculture department's recommended variety list for your district

Wheat___healthy.prevention: Seed treatment with Vitavax Power @ 2.5g/kg seed. Balanced fertilization. Monitor from tillering stage.
